item.title: findash 1.4.3
item.description:
What’s new in 1.4.3

  • Fewer “too many requests” errors: when Yahoo Finance rate-limits, panels now retry briefly and back off together instead of flashing an error — they keep showing the last good data and recover on their own.
  • Opens in full screen: findash now launches borderless, filling the screen. Toggle it with Shift+F11 or under Settings ▸ Full Screen.

item.title: findash 1.4.2
item.description:
What’s new in 1.4.2

  • Single instance: findash now runs as one window. Launching it again — from the taskbar, Start menu, or desktop — brings the existing window to the front instead of opening a duplicate, and re-surfaces it when it’s tucked away in the system tray.

item.title: findash 1.4.1
item.description:
What’s new in 1.4.1

  • New Settings menu: everything except Panels now lives in one place — theme, layouts, API keys, the guide, updates, and quit.
  • Color-blind mode (Settings ▸ Color-blind mode): deuteranopia-safe up/down colors plus ▲/▼ direction marks, so gains and losses read without relying on red/green.
  • Turn on Close to tray right from Settings (previously only on the tray icon).
  • Visual refresh across dark and light: thinner panel dividers, a crisp new title-bar icon set (including a clearer maximize icon), sharper corners, and subtle hover feedback.
  • News: the article preview now closes when you click off the News panel.

item.title: findash 1.4.0
item.description:
What’s new in 1.4.0

  • Data tables now sort (click a header), filter (type in the box above), and let you show/hide columns — Watchlist, Movers, Holders, Earnings, and more.
  • Charts: a crosshair with an O/H/L/C readout as you hover, plus drawing tools — trendlines and channels (parallel, flat-bottom, disjoint) that snap to candles and save with your layout.
  • News: filter headlines, read ones dim, and a single click shows a preview snippet.
  • Command bar: press ↑/↓ for history, Tab to autocomplete, and run slash-commands (/add, /layout, /save, /refresh).
  • Options Chain: Black–Scholes Greeks (Δ, Γ, Θ, Vega) as hideable columns, with plain-language tooltips.
  • Portfolio: new Allocation, Performance (vs SPY, measured from your buy dates), and Risk tabs; record buy/sell dates and leave price on “market” to use the live quote.
  • Price alerts with system-tray notifications, and Undo (Ctrl+Z) for your edits.

item.title: findash 1.3.4
item.description:
What’s new in 1.3.4

  • Under-the-hood hardening: unexpected errors are now written to a local findash.log so problems can be diagnosed, and the data scheduler resolves feed timings faster.

item.title: findash 1.3.3
item.description:
What’s new in 1.3.3

  • Offline-friendly startup: recently-seen quotes, charts, and tables now appear instantly on the next launch — even with no connection — while fresh data loads in the background.
  • The F1 “Keyboard Shortcuts & Guide” now lists the newer shortcuts (Ctrl+W close panel, Ctrl+Shift+T reopen last closed, Ctrl+1–4 link group) and the light/dark theme toggle.

item.title: findash 1.3.2
item.description:
What’s new in 1.3.2

  • Light theme polish: charts now follow the theme (no more black chart background in light mode), table headers stand out, and low-contrast elements were darkened for readability.
  • New keyboard shortcuts: Ctrl+W closes the focused panel, Ctrl+Shift+T reopens the last one you closed, and Ctrl+1–4 set the focused panel’s link group (A/B/C/D).
  • Fixed a memory build-up when repeatedly adding and removing chart indicators.
  • Layout files saved by a newer version are now declined gracefully instead of loading incorrectly.

item.title: findash 1.3.1
item.description:
What’s new in 1.3.1

  • New light theme — switch under View ▸ Theme (dark stays the default; the app restarts to apply it).
  • This update window now shows dark text on a white background, so release notes are easy to read.
  • Reliability: data fetches that stall now time out and report an error instead of hanging a panel; the options chain ignores late data from a previously-typed symbol; layouts and saved API keys are written atomically so they can’t be corrupted by a crash mid-save.

item.title: findash 1.3.0
item.description:
What’s new in 1.3.0

  • Export any data table to CSV — right-click a table and choose “Export Table to CSV…” (Movers, Holders, Dividends, Earnings, Analyst Recs, Commodities, FX Monitor, Macro, Financials).
  • Press Ctrl+F (Cmd+F on macOS) to jump to the SYMBOL box with its text selected, ready to overtype.
  • New Keyboard Shortcuts & Guide window — shown once on first launch, and any time via F1 or Help ▸ Keyboard Shortcuts & Guide… — with a full hotkey cheat sheet and a dashboard walkthrough.
  • Data tables now show a “Loading…” overlay while fetching.
  • Under the hood: faster news sorting, tighter cleanup of data subscriptions when panels close, and a failing panel now reports to the status bar instead of failing silently.

item.title: findash 1.2.3
item.description: • Chart: hovering an entry in the indicator "+" menu (SMA, EMA, BB, VWAP, VOL, RSI, MACD) now shows a tooltip with its full name and a one-line description.

item.title: findash 1.2.2
item.description: • Fixed the indicator chip remove button added in 1.2.1, which rendered as an empty box instead of an × on some systems — removal is back to right-click a chip → Remove (also still reachable via Color…/Edit period…).

item.title: findash 1.2.1
item.description:
• Chart: pick a custom color when adding any indicator (SMA, EMA, Bollinger Bands, VWAP, Volume, RSI, MACD), instead of only an auto-assigned one.
  • Chart: each indicator chip now has a small × button for one-click removal — previously only available via the right-click menu.

item.title: findash 1.2.0
item.description:
• Press F5 to force-refresh every live feed at once — quotes, charts, news, financials — with status-bar feedback.
  • Financials: open the company's SEC filings (EDGAR, matched to Annual/Quarterly) or Yahoo Finance; download the displayed statement as a styled Excel workbook or clean CSV; copy it to the clipboard.
  • Chart: candlestick, OHLC bar, line, and area styles; add/remove/recolor indicators (SMA, EMA, Bollinger Bands, VWAP, MACD, RSI, volume); custom colors for candles, line, grid, and background.
  • Chart: pick range and candle interval independently — including a custom date range — with invalid combinations greyed out and explained; export the chart as PNG; everything persists with your layout.
  • The app now launches maximized; volume axis shows compact figures (150M, 2.5B); fixed clipped range/interval button labels.

item.title: findash 1.1.0
item.description:
• Connect API keys in-app: new APIs menu shows each data source's connection status and opens a connect dialog — free-key links included, no .env editing needed. Keys apply immediately, no restart.
  • First launch of a new install or update now offers a one-time setup prompt when no keys are connected yet.
  • As always, everything keeps working with no keys — free keyless sources remain the default.

item.title: findash 1.0.3
item.description:
• News: headlines now come from major publisher feeds first (Bloomberg, WSJ, MarketWatch, CNBC, and more) — fresher and broader than before, with the previous sources kept as fallback.
  • News: the feed is now sorted — headlines mentioning your active symbol rise to the top, newest first within each group.
  • Quotes: optional Finnhub / Twelve Data sources via FINNHUB_API_KEY / TWELVEDATA_API_KEY in .env; without keys, quotes work exactly as before.

item.title: findash 1.0.2
item.description: • Fix: the app now reopens correctly after an auto-update (the post-update relaunch waits for files to settle).

item.title: findash 1.0.1
item.description: • Share layouts with others: export a saved layout to a .findashlayout file, and import files straight into your saved layouts (Layout menu).
